title: BlackBerry Stock Analysis and Valuation
text: BlackBerry analysis suggests the company is undergoing a significant turnaround with improving fundamentals and strong growth prospects. The company's QNX division is embedded in 275 million vehicles globally with expanding applications in robotics and industrial automation. Management cited the turnaround as complete and positioned the company as a growth story. The company achieved the Rule of 40 status, indicating strong profitability and growth balance. Financial metrics show eight consecutive quarters of improving profitability with Q4 2026 net income of $24.3 million and full-year 2026 net income of $53.2 million. Revenue growth forecasts of 6-11% for FY2027 reflect normalized expectations post-turnaround. The company's 87% net retention rate in cybersecurity and strong QNX royalty backlog of $950 million suggest durable revenue streams. Valuation appears reasonable given growth prospects and cash position of $283 million. The company's transformation from software patent licensing to operational software and services company represents a fundamental business improvement.
